mechanism_narrative: A stable or gently rising FEDFUNDS rate near 4.5% in early 2006 reflects a Fed nearing the end of its tightening cycle. As policy rate increases decelerate, equity discount-rate pressure eases, producing modestly positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days as markets price in a pause.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 4.49% in Feb 2006 after prolonged tightening from 1% in 2004
failure_condition: If 20-day forward SPX returns following months of stable/rising FEDFUNDS show no statistically significant positive skew (t-test p>0.05) over 2005-2008 walk-forward window, or Rank IC stays below 0.03 across rolling 2-year windows, reject hypothesis.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in UNRATE of more than 0.3 percentage points within a 3-month window signals an inflection toward labor market deterioration, historically preceding broader earnings downgrades and risk-off equity positioning, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the subsequent quarter.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises more than 0.3pp over trailing 3 months
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 4.8% Feb 2006, near cycle lows
failure_condition: If triggered episodes show SPX 60-day forward returns positive in more than 55% of instances across 2005-2020 sample, or Rank IC < 0.03 in rolling 3-year windows, reject hypothesis.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) weakness combined with elevated FEDFUNDS jointly signal that tight monetary policy is suppressing interest-rate-sensitive demand sectors; the interaction of declining housing activity under a high policy rate regime predicts negative forward equity returns as this dual pressure compounds credit and consumption channels beyond either signal alone.
candidate_trigger: HOUST declines >5% over 3 months while FEDFUNDS remains above 4%
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 2273K (revised) with FEDFUNDS at 4.49% in early 2006
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle with housing slowdown
failure_condition: If joint-trigger episodes fail to produce more negative average 20-day forward returns than single-factor HOUST-only triggers (difference not significant at p<0.10) across 2005-2008 sample, reject interaction hypothesis in favor of additive model.

mechanism_narrative: In a late-cycle expansion characterized by low UNRATE and steady payroll growth, continued PAYEMS gains above trend indicate labor market tightness that raises wage-cost concerns and Fed hawkishness risk, muting further equity upside and producing weaker-than-baseline 60-day forward S&P 500 returns compared to mid-cycle expansions.
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS at 135733K revised, UNRATE at 4.8% indicating tight labor market in early 2006
failure_condition: If PAYEMS strength during low-UNRATE late-cycle periods correlates with positive rather than negative 60-day forward returns (opposite sign, p<0.05) across 2005-2007 sample, reject hypothesis.

mechanism_narrative: Positive month-over-month growth in INDPRO reflects expanding industrial output and corporate production momentum, which mechanically feeds into near-term earnings expectations and produces positive S&P 500 returns over the following trading week as markets react to incoming production data releases.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO reported at 98.20 (revised) trending upward in late-cycle expansion
failure_condition: If 5-day forward returns following positive INDPRO monthly prints show no significant difference from returns following negative prints (p>0.10) over 2005-2015 sample, reject hypothesis.

mechanism_narrative: A deceleration in RSAFS (retail sales) growth below its trailing 6-month average signals weakening consumer demand, a leading indicator of GDP slowdown, and predicts negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month as analysts revise consumer discretionary earnings downward.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS month-over-month growth falls below trailing 6-month average by more than 1 standard deviation
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS reported at 347021M revised value for Feb 2006
failure_condition: If triggered RSAFS deceleration episodes show SPX 20-day forward returns positive in more than 55% of cases across 2005-2019 sample, or Rank IC < 0.03 over rolling 3-year windows, reject hypothesis.

mechanism_narrative: The interaction between elevated FEDFUNDS and rising CPIAUCSL captures a compressed real interest rate environment; when nominal rates rise faster than inflation, the resulting increase in real rates raises the equity discount rate disproportionately, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over a quarterly horizon that neither series predicts alone.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS minus trailing 12-month CPIAUCSL inflation rate exceeds 2 percentage points
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 4.49% vs CPIAUCSL 199.3 index level implying moderate real rate in early 2006
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle with rising real rates
failure_condition: If real-rate interaction signal fails to outperform standalone FEDFUNDS-level model in explaining 60-day forward return variance (incremental R^2 < 0.01) across 2005-2010 walk-forward test, reject interaction hypothesis.

mechanism_narrative: During a monetary tightening cycle, a sharp month-over-month decline in HOUST signals immediate rate-sensitivity stress in housing markets that historically leads short-term equity volatility spikes, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the very near-term 5-day horizon before broader market recognition and repricing.
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 2273K revised amid FEDFUNDS tightening cycle from 1% to 4.5%
failure_condition: If HOUST declines during tightening regimes show no significant negative 5-day forward return effect (p>0.10) relative to HOUST declines during easing regimes, reject regime-conditional claim.
